steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The store starts with 700 customers in January 2018 ($m=0$) and grows 8% per month. An 8% monthly increase means each month's count is multiplied by $1.08$. The model is $c = 700(1.08)^m$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $c = 700(0.08)^m$: $0.08$ is just the rate, not the growth factor; this decays to zero. ✗
  • Choice B — $c = 700(1.08)^m$: starting value 700, growth factor $1 + 0.08 = 1.08$, exponent $m$ months. ✓
  • Choice C — $c = 700(1.8)^m$: uses a growth factor of 1.8, which would mean an 80% monthly increase, not 8%. ✗
  • Choice D — $c = 700(8)^m$: uses a growth factor of 8, which is far larger than an 8% increase. ✗
That matches Choice B — $c = 700(1.08)^m$.
